statement: Express \(\pi^2\) as a decimal correct to \( 3 \) significant figures.
If \(2^x \cdot 8^x = 64\), then \(x =\)
If \(\dfrac{a+x}{b+x} = \dfrac{c}{d}\) (\(c \neq d\)), then \(x =\)
If \(\log(x+a) = 2\), then \(x =\)
If \(2x^2 + x + m\) is divisible by \(x-2\), then it is also divisible by

Which of the following is/are an identity/identities?
I. \(x^2 = 4\)
II. \((2x+3)^2 = 4x^2+12x+9\)
III. \((x+1)^2 = x^2+1\)
Solve \(\begin{cases} \dfrac{3}{x} - y = 1 \\ 2y - \dfrac{1}{2x} = 1 \end{cases}\)
Which of the following systems of inequalities has its solution represented by the shaded region in the figure?

There are \( 1\,200 \) students in a school, of which \( 640 \) are boys and \( 560 \) are girls. If \( 55 \% \) of the boys and \( 40 \% \) of the girls wear glasses, what percentage of students in the school wear glasses?
In a map of scale \(1:500\), the length and breadth of a rectangular field are \( 2 \text{ cm}\) and \( 3 \text{ cm}\) respectively. Find the actual area of this field.
In the figure, \(\sin \theta + \tan \theta =\)
In the figure, find \(\theta\) correct to the nearest degree.
In the figure, the square sandwich \(ABCD\) is cut into two equal halves along \(EF\) so that \(AE : ED = 2 : 1\). Find \(\theta\) correct to the nearest degree.
In the figure, the area of \(\Delta ABC\) is \( 18 \). Find \(\angle ABC\) correct to the nearest degree.
In the figure, \(BEA\) is a semicircle. \(ABCD\) is a rectangle and \(DC\) touches the semicircle at \(E\). Find the area of the shaded region.
In the figure, find \(x\).
In the figure, \(BCA\) is a semicircle. If \(AC = 6\) and \(CB = 4\), find the area of the semicircle.
In the figure, \(ABCDE\) is a regular pentagon and \(ABF\) is an equilateral triangle. Find \(\theta\).
In the figure, \(EC\) is the tangent to the circle at \(C\). Find \(\angle CBD\).
In the figure, find the area of \(\Delta ABC\).
Which of the following lines is perpendicular to the line \(\dfrac{x}{2} + \dfrac{y}{3} = 1\)?
In the pie chart, if \(x : y : z = 75 : 106 : 119\), find \(x\).
The histogram below shows the distribution of the weights of \( 30 \) students. Find the mean weight of these students.
Two fair dice are thrown. Find the probability that the sum of the two numbers shown is \( 8 \).
In a test, there are 3 questions. For each question, the probability that John correctly answers it is \(\dfrac{2}{5}\). Find the probability that he gets exactly \( 2 \) questions correct.
Simplify \(\dfrac{4}{x^2-4} - \dfrac{3}{x^2-x-2}\).
\(\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}-1} - \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}} =\)
The difference of the roots of the equation \(2x^2-5x+k=0\) is \(\dfrac{7}{2}\). Find \(k\).
In the figure, find the coordinates of the mid-point of \(AB\).
Find the values of \(x\) which satisfy both \(-2x  \lt  3\) and \((x+3)(x-2)  \lt  0\).

If \(a \lt b \lt 0\), then which of the following must be true?
I. \(a^2 \lt b^2\)
II. \(ab \lt a^2\)
III. \(\dfrac{1}{a} \lt \dfrac{1}{b}\)
The figure shows the graph of a quadratic function \(f(x)\). If the vertex of the graph is (1, 3), then \(f(x) =\)
The \(n\)-th term of an arithmetic sequence is \(3+2n\). Find the sum of the first \( 50 \) terms of the sequence.
The first term of a geometric sequence is \(a\). If the sum to infinity of the sequence is \(\dfrac{3}{4}a\), then its common ratio is

\(a, b, c, d\) are 4 consecutive terms of a geometric sequence. Which of the following must be true?
I. \(b^2 = ac\)
II. \(\dfrac{b}{a} = \dfrac{d}{c}\)
III. \(\dfrac{d}{a} = \left(\dfrac{c}{b}\right)^3\)
Find the interest on \( \$ 10 \) 000 at \( 16 \% \) per annum for \( 2 \) years, compounded half-yearly. Give the answer correct to the nearest dollar.
Suppose \(x\) varies directly as \(y\) and inversely as \(z\). When \(y=2\) and \(z=3\), \(x=7\). When \(y=6\) and \(z=7\), \(x =\)
\(\dfrac{\cos(90^\circ-A)\sin(180^\circ-A)}{\tan(360^\circ-A)} =\)
In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a rectangle inclined at an angle of \(45^\circ\) to the horizontal plane \(BCEF\). Find the inclination of \(AC\) to the horizontal plane correct to the nearest degree.
In the figure, \(CD =\)
For \(0 \le \theta \le 2\pi\), how many roots does the equation \(\tan \theta (\tan \theta - 2) = 0\) have?
In the figure, \(f(x) =\)

The equation of a circle is given by \(x^2+y^2-4x+6y-3=0\). Which of the following statements is/are true?
I. The centre of the circle is (-2, 3).
II. The radius of the circle is \( 4 \).
III. The origin is inside the circle.

A circle has \((a, 0)\) and \((0, b)\) as the endpoints of a diameter. Which of the following points lie(s) on this circle?
I. \((-a, -b)\)
II. \((0, 0)\)
III. \((a, b)\)

In the figure, \(AEB\) and \(ADC\) are straight lines. \(ED \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} BC\) and \(ED : BC = 2 : 3\). If the coordinates of \(A\) and \(B\) are (4, 7) and (0, 1) respectively, find the coordinates of \(E\).
In the figure, \(OXY\) is a sector with centre \(O\). If \(Z\) is the mid-point of \(YO\), find area of \(\Delta OXZ :\) area of sector \(OXY\).
In the figure, the rocket model consists of three parts. Parts I and III can be joined together to form a right circular cone. Part II is a right cylinder. Find the volume of the rocket model.

In the figure, \(AC\) is the angle bisector of \(\angle BAD\). Which of the following statements must be true?
I. \(\Delta BCE \sim \Delta ADE\)
II. \(\Delta ABC \sim \Delta AED\)
III. \(\Delta ABC \sim \Delta BDA\)
In the figure, \(\overparen{AB}=2\), \(\overparen{BC}=3\), \(\overparen{CD}=4\) and \(\overparen{DA}=6\). Find \(\angle BCD\).
In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a parallelogram. \(PDC\), \(PQRS\) and \(ABS\) are straight lines. If \(AQ = 4\), \(QD = 2\) and \(BR = RC = 3\), then \(PQ : QR : RS =\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a rectangle. \(CDE\) is a straight line and \(AE \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} BD\). If the area of \(ABCD\) is \( 24 \) and \(F\) is a point on \(BC\) such that \(BF : FC = 3 : 1\), find the area of \(\Delta DEF\).
In the figure, \(AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} DC\). If the areas of \(\Delta ABE\) and \(\Delta CDE\) are \( 4 \) and \( 9 \) respectively, find the area of \(\Delta BCE\).
